---
title: "nitrix.stats.gp"
generated: true
signature-hash: 697769ddc887cf4e
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub ‚Äî fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.gp`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC ¬ß6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map ‚Äî voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `3` | *(undocumented ‚Äî add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations ‚Äî time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `g` | *(undocumented ‚Äî add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `GPResult` {#GPResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GPResult -->
```python
class GPResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:GPResult -->
Per-element Gaussian-process fit output (`gp_fit`).

Some fields are engine- or mode-conditional: `lo` / `hi` / `boundary`
drive `engine='hsgp'` re-evaluation only; `nd_meta` is set only for a
multi-dimensional fit (`None` otherwise); `corr_rho` is `0` unless a
`corr` structure was fitted; and for a non-Gaussian `family` `coef` is
on the link scale (see :func:`gp_predict`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GPResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(coef: Float[Array, 'V p'], cov_unscaled: Float[Array, 'V p p'], theta: Float[Array, 'V 3'], log_mlik: Float[Array, 'V'], edf: Float[Array, 'V'], dispersion: Float[Array, 'V'], corr_rho: Float[Array, 'V'], kernel: str, engine: str, corr: str, n_obs: int, rank: int, n_fixed: int, lo: float, hi: float, boundary: float, nd_meta: Optional[Any], family: str = 'gaussian') -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_fit` {#gp_fit}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_fit -->
```python
gp_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], x: Union[Float[Array, ' N'], Float[Array, 'N D']], *, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None, kernel: str = 'matern52', rank: Optional[Any] = None, boundary: float = 1.5, bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, engine: Literal['hsgp', 'exact'] = 'hsgp', select: Literal['shared-rho'] = 'shared-rho', ard: bool = False, rho_bounds: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, n_rho: int = 24, map_rho: Optional[Callable[[Float[Array, '']], Float[Array, '']]] = None, corr: Optional[Any] = None, group: Optional[Any] = None, time: Optional[Float[Array, ' N']] = None, n_corr: int = 9, corr_raw_bounds: Tuple[float, float] = (-4.0, 4.0), family: Union[str, Family] = 'gaussian', prior_weights: Optional[Float[Array, ' N']] = None, n_pql: int = 8, n_outer: int = 30, n_search: int = 15, ridge: float = 1e-08, lam_floor: float = 1e-06, lam_ceil: float = 100000000.0, block: Optional[int] = None) -> GPResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_fit -->
Fit a mass-univariate Gaussian-process regression with REML-estimated
lengthscale (Gaussian responses).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` √ó `N` | ‚Äî | `(V, N)` responses (one row per element). |
| `x` | `Union[Float[Array, ' N'], Float[Array, 'N D']]` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | `(N,)` covariate, or `(N, D)` for a **multi-dimensional** GP (a spatial smooth / smooth interaction; HSGP tensor-product engine). This is a single covariate the GP smooths *over* -- **not** the full design matrix `X` of `glm_fit` / `gam_fit`; linear covariates go to `parametric=`. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional `(N, q)` unpenalised linear design (covariates entering linearly alongside the intercept). |
| `kernel` | `str` | ‚Äî | `'matern52'` | Stationary kernel: `'matern52'` (default) / `'matern32'` / `'matern12'` / `'rbf'`. |
| `rank` | `Optional[Any]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Smooth-basis rank `m`. `None` (default) uses an engine-appropriate value: `20` for 1-D `'hsgp'` (small `rho` needs a larger `rank`), `N` for `'exact'` (full-rank), and `8` **per axis** for a multi-dimensional fit. An explicit `rank < N` with `engine='exact'` gives the eigen-truncated Karhunen-Loeve / Nystrom approximation; for multi-D, `rank` may be a per-axis sequence `[m_1, ..., m_D]`. |
| `boundary` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1.5` | Domain-extension factor `L / half-range` (`>= 1`; default `1.5`; `'hsgp'` only). |
| `bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(lo, hi)` data-range override (defaults to the data min/max). |
| `engine` | `Literal['hsgp', 'exact']` | ‚Äî | `'hsgp'` | Reduced-rank engine. `'hsgp'` (default; Hilbert-space eigenfunctions, a fixed design with an `eigh`-free `rho`-dependent diagonal penalty) or `'exact'` (kernel eigenfeatures `U Lambda^{1/2}` -- the full-rank GP when `rank == N`; a one-off host `eigh` of the shared kernel Gram per `rho`, cuSOLVER-free, equivalent to `lme.reml_fit` by the penalty<->variance-component identity). |
| `select` | `Literal['shared-rho']` | ‚Äî | `'shared-rho'` | Lengthscale-selection mode. `'shared-rho'` (the only mode, and the default) shares a single `rho` across all elements with per-element amplitude and noise -- the mass-univariate design that keeps the final fit `N`-free. The parameter is a forward-compatibility hook for a future per-element `rho`. |
| `ard` | `bool` | ‚Äî | `False` | Multi-D only: `False` (default) estimates one **isotropic** lengthscale (a radial kernel of `\|\|x - x'\|\|`); `True` estimates a **per-axis** lengthscale (ARD / separable kernel) by coordinate descent over the axes. |
| `rho_bounds` | `Optional[Tuple[float, float]]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(rho_lo, rho_hi)` search range. Defaults to `(0.05, 2.0) * (hi - lo)`. |
| `n_rho` | `int` | ‚Äî | `24` | Number of log-spaced grid points for the `rho` search. |
| `map_rho` | `Optional[Callable[[Float[Array, '']], Float[Array, '']]]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional callable `rho -> -log p(rho)` adding a lengthscale prior to the pooled objective (a MAP / prior-regularised `rho`); `None` is pure REML. Use a builder from :mod:`nitrix.stats.priors` (`halfnormal_prior` / `invgamma_prior` / `lognormal_prior`) or any pure-JAX callable. |
| `corr` | `Optional[Any]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Within-group residual-correlation structure: `'ar1'` (discrete AR(1)), `'car1'` (continuous-time AR(1); pass `time`), `'cs'` (compound symmetry), or a `lme.CorrSpec`. `None` (default) is the i.i.d. residual. When set, `group` is required; the residual is `sigma_e^2 R(rho_c)` block-diagonal across `group`, with `rho_c` estimated jointly with the lengthscale. |
| `group` | `Optional[Any]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(N,)` integer grouping factor for `corr` (the residual is correlated *within* groups, independent across them). Required when `corr` is set. |
| `time` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' N']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(N,)` observation times for `corr='car1'` (and to order `ar1` when rows are not in within-group time order). |
| `n_corr` | `int` | ‚Äî | `9` | Number of grid points for the residual-correlation parameter search. |
| `corr_raw_bounds` | `Tuple[float, float]` | ‚Äî | `(-4.0, 4.0)` | `(lo, hi)` range of the structure's *unconstrained* grid parameter (MC5). `ar1` maps it by `tanh` -- the default `(-4, 4)` spans `rho_c in (-0.999, 0.999)`. `car1` / `cs` map it by `sigmoid`, a **one-sided** window `rho_c in (0.018, 0.982)` (`cs` is positive by construction, the common exchangeable regime; `car1` is a positive decay). The raw grid is not parabolically refined, so widen the bounds / raise `n_corr` for a finer estimate; an estimate at the grid edge warns. |
| `family` | `Union[str, Family]` | ‚Äî | `'gaussian'` | ‚Äî |
| `prior_weights` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' N']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | ‚Äî |
| `n_pql` | `int` | ‚Äî | `8` | ‚Äî |
| `n_outer` | `int` | ‚Äî | `30` | Fellner-Schall iterations for the final fit and for each `rho`-search evaluation, respectively. |
| `n_search` | `int` | ‚Äî | `15` | Fellner-Schall iterations for the final fit and for each `rho`-search evaluation, respectively. |
| `ridge` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-08` | Small stabiliser on the penalised normal equations. |
| `lam_floor` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-06` | Clamp on the smoothing parameter `lambda = sigma_e^2 / sigma_f^2`. |
| `lam_ceil` | `float` | ‚Äî | `100000000.0` | Clamp on the smoothing parameter `lambda = sigma_e^2 / sigma_f^2`. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional element-block size bounding peak memory on brain-scale `V`. |

**Returns** `GPResult`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_predict` {#gp_predict}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_predict -->
```python
gp_predict(result: GPResult, x_new: Float[Array, ' g'], *, parametric: Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']] = None, x_train: Optional[Float[Array, ' N']] = None, type: Literal['link', 'response'] = 'link') -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_predict -->
Per-element posterior mean and standard deviation of the GP at `x_new`.

Evaluates `mean = X_new beta` and the predictive variance
`sigma_e^2 diag(X_new V X_new^T)`, where the smooth columns of `X_new` are
the basis re-evaluated at `x_new`: for `engine='hsgp'` the (`rho`-
independent) eigenfunctions, reconstructed from the recorded domain
`(lo, hi, boundary)` and `rank` (self-contained); for `engine='exact'`
the Nystrom kernel features `K(x_new, x_train) U Lambda^{-1/2}`, which need
the original `x_train` (the result does not store the shared training grid
among its per-element fields).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GPResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | A :class:`GPResult` from :func:`gp_fit`. |
| `x_new` | `Float` | `g` | ‚Äî | `(g,)` covariate grid to predict on. |
| `parametric` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'g q']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(g, q)` parametric covariates matching `gp_fit`'s `parametric` (must be supplied when the fit used parametric columns). |
| `x_train` | `Optional[Float[Array, ' N']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(N,)` original training covariate -- **required for `engine='exact'`** (ignored for `'hsgp'`). |
| `type` | `Literal['link', 'response']` | ‚Äî | `'link'` | `'link'` (default) returns the latent linear-predictor `eta`; for a non-Gaussian fit, `'response'` maps the mean through `family.linkinv` (the mean response, e.g. a probability / rate) and scales `std` by the delta-method factor `\|d mu / d eta\|` (a point-estimate response-scale SD). A no-op for the Gaussian (identity-link) fit. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_aic` {#gp_aic}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_aic -->
```python
gp_aic(result: _ICResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_aic -->
Per-element Akaike information criterion `-2 l_R + 2 k` for a GP / HGP
fit (lower is better).

Uses the **REML log marginal likelihood** `log_mlik` (so it is comparable
across kernels / ranks / amplitudes on the same data) and the effective
degrees of freedom `k` (:func:`_total_edf`) as the model complexity -- the
mgcv-style marginal AIC.  Like any REML criterion it is valid for models with
the **same fixed-effect structure** (a different `parametric` design changes
the restriction); within that, it is immediate for GP-vs-GP and GP-vs-spline
(an `hsgp_basis` / kernel choice) selection.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_aic -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `_ICResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `gp_bic` {#gp_bic}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gp_bic -->
```python
gp_bic(result: _ICResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gp_bic -->
Per-element Bayesian information criterion `-2 l_R + k log N` for a GP /
HGP fit (lower is better); see :func:`gp_aic` for the comparability caveat.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gp_bic -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `_ICResult` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
